title: Crepidium umbraticola
taxonomy: Crepidium umbraticola is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest family of flowering plants. The genus Crepidium is a subgenus within the larger genus Pleurothallis. This classification places Crepidium umbraticola within the subfamily Pleurothallidinae. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the orchid family.

intro: Crepidium umbraticola, also known as the Umbrella Orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, where it has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Crepidium umbraticola include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory infections, fever, and other inflammatory conditions.
